description: Fix the Windows CI failure where `opentabs plugin list` exits with code 1 instead of 0 when run in a clean environment (empty config dir, no server running). The CLI smoke test at platform/cli/src/cli-smoke.test.ts:126 consistently fails on Windows CI. The root cause needs to be diagnosed and fixed.

userStories.title: Diagnose and fix opentabs plugin list exit code 1 on Windows
userStories.description: The `opentabs plugin list` command exits with code 1 on Windows CI when run with an empty OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR (no config, no server). The test at cli-smoke.test.ts:126 asserts exit code 0 and consistently fails on Windows. The handlePluginList function in plugin.ts does not explicitly call process.exit(1) — the error propagates through Commander's async error handling to cli.ts line 71: `process.exitCode ??= 1`. This means an unhandled exception is thrown somewhere in the offline code path that only manifests on Windows.
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs plugin list` exits with code 0 on Windows when OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR is an empty temp directory and no server is running
The cli-smoke.test.ts 'opentabs plugin list exits 0' test passes on Windows CI
The fix handles the Windows-specific error gracefully without affecting macOS/Linux behavior
